# GitHub Copilot Configuration for @rtorcato/js-common\n\nThis repository is optimized for GitHub Copilot with the following enhancements:\n\n## 🎯 Copilot Skills Location\n\nThe `.github/skills/` directory contains:\n- `javascript-utilities.md` - Core utility patterns and examples\n- `typescript-patterns.md` - TypeScript-specific patterns and generics\n- `advanced-patterns.md` - Complex compositions and real-world usage\n\n## 🔧 AI-Enhanced Features\n\n### Smart Import Suggestions\nCopilot will suggest appropriate imports based on context:\n```typescript\n// When working with arrays, suggests:\nimport { unique, chunk, groupBy } from '@rtorcato/js-common/arrays'\n\n// When working with strings, suggests:\nimport { camelCase, kebabCase, capitalize } from '@rtorcato/js-common/strings'\n\n// When handling errors, suggests:\nimport { attempt, trycatch } from '@rtorcato/js-common/try'\n```\n\n### Context-Aware Code Completion\n- **Data processing**: Suggests functional composition patterns\n- **Validation**: Recommends validation chains with proper error handling\n- **Async operations**: Proposes error-safe patterns with `attempt`/`trycatch`\n- **Type safety**: Maintains generic types throughout suggestions\n\n### Usage Pattern Recognition\nCopilot recognizes these common patterns:\n\n1. **API Processing Pipeline**:\n   ```typescript\n   const result = await attempt(async () => {\n     return users\n       .filter(user => isEmail(user.email))\n       .map(user => pick(user, ['id', 'name']))\n       .reduce(groupBy, user => user.role)\n   })\n   ```\n\n2. **Input Validation Chain**:\n   ```typescript\n   if (isEmpty(email) || !isEmail(email)) {\n     throw new Error('Invalid email')\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Configuration Management**:\n   ```typescript\n   const config = {\n     port: parseInt(getEnv('PORT', '3000')),\n     apiKey: requireEnv('API_KEY')\n   }\n   ```\n\n## 📚 Enhanced Documentation\n\n### JSDoc with Examples\nEvery function includes:\n- **Purpose**: Clear description of what the function does\n- **Examples**: Multiple usage examples with expected outputs\n- **Type information**: Full TypeScript generics and constraints\n- **Performance notes**: Algorithmic complexity where relevant\n- **Related functions**: Cross-references to complementary utilities\n\n### Code Categories\nFunctions are categorized for better discovery:\n- `@category Array Utilities`\n- `@category String Utilities`\n- `@category Object Utilities`\n- `@category Validation`\n- `@category Error Handling`\n\n## 🚀 Performance Awareness\n\nCopilot is trained on performance characteristics:\n- Array operations use optimal algorithms (Set for `unique`, Fisher-Yates for `shuffle`)\n- Batch processing patterns with `chunk` for large datasets\n- Memory-efficient object operations\n- Zero-dependency core utilities\n\n## 🔒 Security Patterns\n\nCopilot will suggest secure patterns:\n- Input validation before processing\n- Safe environment variable handling\n- Cryptographic operations with strong algorithms\n- Error handling that doesn't leak sensitive information\n\n## 🎯 Best Practices Integration\n\nCopilot promotes:\n- **Functional composition** over imperative loops\n- **Type safety** with TypeScript generics\n- **Error handling** with try utilities\n- **Immutability** - utilities return new objects/arrays\n- **Tree-shaking** - specific imports over namespace imports\n\n## 🤖 AI Training Data\n\nThis repository includes:\n- **Usage examples** in JSDoc comments\n- **Integration patterns** in skills documentation\n- **Error scenarios** with proper handling\n- **Performance benchmarks** for optimization guidance\n- **Real-world use cases** from common development scenarios\n\nThe combination of comprehensive documentation, usage patterns, and performance awareness helps GitHub Copilot provide more accurate and useful suggestions for developers using `@rtorcato/js-common`.
